methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a Series C defense and security company founded by Even Rogers, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B against $420M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 10 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, comprised of 8 private and 2 public comparables, with valuations ranging from $500M to $9.2B.

Two triangulation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a typical 1.7x multiplier to the current $1.5B post-money valuation, implying $2.5B forward value. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a sample distribution including Saronic and Chaos Industries at Series D valuations of $9.2B and $4.5B respectively, and Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI clustered around $1.4–$1.5B at Series B. The wide dispersion ($500M low to $9.2B high) reflects heterogeneity in stage and market positioning within the defense-tech sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.9B (high), with a median point estimate of $2.0B. The current $1.5B valuation sits toward the lower bound. Key uncertainties include stage-level step-up comparability in an emerging defense-tech market and the influence of outlier peers (Saronic, Chaos Industries) on peer-set medians. Additional clarity on True Anomaly's revenue scale and customer concentration relative to named comparables would refine this range.
